observations.recommendation: Create a committee that will rigorously pursue the collection of prior year receivables from the identified debtors. Relative to this, Management shall exhaust all possible efforts to collect the receivables such as, but not limited to instituting legal actions and sending of demand letters against delinquent borrowers, as applicable.

Revisit the supply and property management system of the agency and require the unit supply officers to render report as regards the issuances and consumption of the inventories within their respective offices to be submitted timely to the GSO/Municipal Treasurer for checking and consolidation prior to submission to Municipal Accountant for proper recording;

Henceforth, instruct the GSO-Designate and Inspectorate team, as applicable, to timely provide the Municipal Accountant the documents such as Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR), Purchase Order and Delivery Receipts, for every duly delivered item, in whole or part. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry to recognize the corresponding asset and liability account with its equivalent amount per Purchase Orders/Contracts;
Require the proper utilization of the stock cards at the office level in the monitoring of supplies and materials.

Create and direct the Agency’s disposal committee to initiate the disposal procedures for the unserviceable property identified costing ₱4,940,787.10 which shall be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP);

Ensure that goods and services that have been delivered or rendered as at year end be properly recorded as liabilities. Relative to this, require, as year-end cut-off procedures, the Municipal General Services Office to forward to the Municipal Accounting Office all claims for delivered goods and services for the proper recognition of Accounts Payable on the related transactions;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to investigate/analyze the nature and status of the transactions covered by the long outstanding accounts totaling ₱1,597,630.56 taking into account the availability of supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall notify the concerned suppliers and contractors about their outstanding receivables from the Agency. Payments shall be made immediately for transactions with complete documentation. Transactions with incomplete supporting documents shall be reverted to unappropriated surplus. Accordingly, unsupported claims shall be closed to Government Equity in the Agency’s books;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the NFS the contingent liability arising from the undelivered POs at year-end to ensure the fair presentation of the FS
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Review the status of the accounts particularly those that are long outstanding for an undetermined period and observe the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of Dormant Payable accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016;

Investigate the exact source of the outstanding balances with no available information, cause the immediate reconciliation of unliquidated fund transfers from the NGAs, and prepare adjusting entries as appropriate;

Strengthen needs-based GAD planning by conducting thorough research and analysis and close coordination with stakeholders and other offices within the agency prior to crafting the programs, project and activities (PPAs) included in the GPB;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Utilize the GAD tools available such as HGDG. Relative to this, include within the PPAs the training program on the HGDG or seek assistance of gender analysis specialist/expert to further increase utilization rate and maximize budget allotment at PPA level;

Develop substitute PPAs in case of unforeseen circumstances that may hinder the implementation of the originally planned PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget, and strictly follow the process flow of approval and endorsement of the updated GAD Plan and Budget as prescribed under Section C.7 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2013-01;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Regularly monitor and evaluate the utilization of the GAD funds that would ensure that total reported costs of GAD accomplishments would achieve at least 5% of agency’s total appropriations/approved budget;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Comply and submit the appropriate documentary requirements in accordance with PCW‐NEDA‐DBM Joint Circular 2013‐01.

Ensure that PPAs funded out of the DRRM funds are well-planned and meticulously reviewed by the LDRRMC. Accordingly, intensive planning and coordination with stakeholders and implementing offices shall be conducted prior to the inclusion of PPAs in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Local Finance Committee, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the PPAs to determine on a timely basis the possible declaration of savings, which will be reappropriated for new or existing LDRRM-related PPAs, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Utilize the stock card in the inventory management to ensure that welfare goods are available and in optimal condition when required. Similarly, ensure the timely and proper disposal of nearly expiring goods/items through the identified modality by the LGU, to preclude any wastage of government funds; and
Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage

Require the Local Disaster Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to review the existing PPAs under the LDRRMF to determine the relevance and responsiveness of the projects to the present circumstances including the reasonableness of project costing and make the necessary revisions as warranted subject to the appropriate approvals;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: Strictly monitor the implementation of the PPAs to facilitate the timely completion thereof;
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to encourage the government officials and employees to settle immediately the disallowances as well as ND with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Perform cash cut-off procedures to ensure the completeness of transactions to be included in the FS.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that Reports of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) are prepared for all undeposited cash collections as of December 31 and furnish these reports to the Accounting Office for proper recognition.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accounting Office to conduct review and assessment of the dormant “Loans Receivable-Others” account and record an allowance for impairment with the following entries:
Demand from the concerned official the immediate and full liquidation of the outstanding CA aged below ten years; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly review and monitor the status of the receivable accounts and observe the guidelines and procedures in the proper disposition of dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Inspectorate Team to conduct immediately an independent and thorough inspection of the reported work accomplished to validate the submitted SWA submitted by F. Casalme Construction & Supply. Based on the findings of the inspection, determine whether the value of the physical accomplishment is commensurate with the 15% mobilization advance granted. If the actual accomplishment is less than the advance, require the contractor to refund the excess amount; and
Instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to conduct a comprehensive review of historical accounting records, contracts, and disbursement vouchers to determine the origin and purpose of the ₱10,000.00 balance under the “Advances to Contractors” account.

We recommended that Management:
Require the OIC–Municipal Accountant to record the issuance of ₱0.309 million worth of welfare goods per the LDRRM Summary Report. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to submit the corresponding RPCI. Henceforth, the Committee shall ensure the conduct of the required semi-annual physical counts and the timely submission of the RPCI to the COA on or before July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management:
Require the OIC–Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balance of the “Accumulated Depreciation – Road Networks” account. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared to present the restated CY 2024 figures for the related “Depreciation Expense” and “Accumulated Depreciation” accounts in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Direct the Inventory Committee and the OIC–Municipal Accountant to reconcile the inventory count report with the accounting records, investigate and evaluate the identified discrepancies, and adjust the records accordingly.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to:
Exert efforts to identify the projects with lacking details, determine its commencement, completion and status. Otherwise, include these items in the one-time cleansing of PPE balances following the procedures and guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
Expedite the initiation of the formal takeover process for the terminated project to safeguard government assets and prevent further delays;
Advise the Municipal Engineer and the Inspectorate Team to issue a Certificate of Completion and an Inspection Report after careful evaluation and inspection of each successfully completed project and transmit the same, together with other integral documents evidencing to the Accounting Office for proper recording; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify, based on sufficient documentation, the fully completed projects from CIP to the appropriate PPE accounts. Also, develop and enforce internal controls and monitoring mechanisms to ensure timely reclassification of completed projects from CIP to PPE and proper recognition of obligations to contractors and suppliers; and
Conduct a thorough review and validation of supporting documents related to the negative balances. If these balances pertain to completed projects, identify the corresponding projects and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure accuracy and proper classification.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Inventory Committee to strictly implement the conduct of physical count of biological assets and the preparation of report thereon which shall be furnished to the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Agriculture Office for recording, reconciliation and monitoring; and
Direct the Municipal Agriculture Office to conduct re-appraisals before year-end and submit the report thereon to the Municipal Accountant to provide ample time for the updating of the balances in the agency’s books.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare working paper adjustment entries for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to review the nature of each transaction with due consideration on the expense recognition principles set by the applicable IPSAS and COA rules and regulations and use the correct account titles.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to investigate and reconstruct the detailed breakdown of the unremitted tax dues, prepare the corresponding 'AlphaList,' and ensure the immediate remittance of the said amounts without further delay.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the immediate reconciliation of the “Due to Pag-Ibig” account to determine whether the ₱44,901.53 balance represents a valid obligation and to establish the accuracy of the account. Once confirmed, appropriate adjustments or payments should be made without further delay. Additionally, a periodic reconciliation process should be instituted to ensure that remittances are accurately and promptly recorded, preventing the accumulation of unresolved balances in future periods.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that all PPAs are well-planned by providing a rigid review and research of specific guidelines set by law on GAD-related issues and concerns;
Regularly monitor and review the implementation of GAD PPAs and make adjustments as necessary in case of unexpected circumstances, following the guidelines prescribed under JMC No. 2013-01; and
Henceforth, strictly ensure the effective and efficient implementation of GAD program, projects and activities to fully utilize the GPB.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the MDRRMC and the LDC to ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned by providing rigid review and evaluation, especially during the preparation of the budget proposal; and
Require the MDRRMC to conduct regular review and monitoring of the implementation of PPAs and update the PPMP as necessary to ensure that funds are fully utilized. Relative to this, strictly ensure the effective and efficient implementation of program, projects and activities to ensure that the fund’s purpose of strengthening the agency’s capabilities in mitigating, responding, and recovering from the impact of disasters are satisfactorily attained.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant inform and require the concerned payees to submit the stale checks issued totaling ₱8,375.72 for replacement, as warranted. Accordingly, an adjusting entry shall be prepared to revert the same to the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Letters were sent to the concerned payees, but no checks were returned as of date.

Adjusting Entries were prepared.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough assessment, evaluation and investigation of the receivables accounts and cause the preparation and maintenance of a detailed aging of receivables accounts;

From the prepared aging of receivables, direct the Municipal Accountant to review the status of the collectability of the dormant receivables and observe the guidelines and procedures on the recognition of impairment or write-off of dormant receivable accounts as prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, as necessary;

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Financial Analyst of the DSWD and request for a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) used in recording the LCC received from the LGU. Accordingly, a receivable shall be set up on the balance as at year-end amounting to ₱661,570.05 and require the periodic liquidation of the amount transferred to the BDC-TWG of Barangay Sinakan.
Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the cost of the issued inventories covered by the 52 SSMIs as noted above and prepare the appropriate accounting entries thereon;

Consider reorganizing the Inventory Committee by creating sub-committees on a “per account title” basis with counterparts from the Accounting Office. Actual physical count, investigation of differences and reconciliation of balances shall be conducted by the sub-committees to be accomplished within a specific timeline to be set. Accordingly, adjust the appropriate records as warranted and submit a revised RPCPPE based on the reconciliations made;

Henceforth, schedule the annual physical count during the current year. Relative to this, strict monitoring and control sha ll be made for the issuances and procurement from the actual date of the count to the December 31 cut-off date for inclusion in the final report to be submitted on or before January 31 of the ensuing year;

Require the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal General Services office to reconcile the noted variance amounting to ₱2,526,003.37 to enhance fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements;

Require the Inventory Committee in coordination with the concerned offices and the Accounting Office to analyze the nature of the items included in the RPCPPE with a total cost of ₱1,301,000.00 and classify them according to the appropriate and specific PPE class for correctness of report submissions;

Direct the Municipal General Service Office to observe the proper inventory taking, naming and property card monitoring of all procured, donated and transferred PPE where the LGU-Sabtang is the rightful owner as prescribed in Chapter 7 of MNGAS-LGUs. Relative to this, all PPE movements (additions/deductions) shall be promptly communicated with the Accounting Office for proper recording/adjustments in the books;
Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain Ledger Cards for each category of PPE;

Prioritize the completion of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances and furnish the Audit Team with the necessary documentation.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate journal entries relative to the unrecorded liabilities totaling ₱93,139.50. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct Calendar Year (CY) 2023 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2024 comparative financial statements;
observations.managementAction: JEV was already prepared

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following adjusting entry to correct the erroneous recording of terminal leave benefits amounting to ₱429,445.14;
observations.managementAction: JEV was already prepared for the adjustment

observations.recommendation: Reclassify the payables due to the PGB totaling ₱4,050,201.64 from the “Due to NGAs” account to the “Due to LGUs” account;
observations.managementAction: JEV for the reclassification was prepared

observations.recommendation: Promptly determine the causes of the negative balances in the Due to NGAs account. Accordingly, Management shall coordinate with the SAs for the reconciliation of the balances and effect the necessary adjusting entries, as warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide in the NFS for the year-ended December 31,2023 the required disclosures as detailed and presented in the Revisions/Remarks column above and in conformity with the requirements of IPSAS 1 to enhance the understandability and reliability of the financial statements.

Provide an explanation/ justification why the ₱1,250,000.00 representing the, of ₱960,484.66 in cash and ₱289,515.34 in-kind should not be disallowed in audit in view of the absence of express authority granted to the Municipal Mayor by the Sangguniang Bayan of LGU-Sabtang to enter into a Tripartite MOA/sub-project agreement with the DSWD and Barangay Sinakan;
Henceforth, ensure that the Municipal Mayor is duly authorized to represent the LGU in all agreements and contracts, as applicable in the circumstances;

Provide an explanation/justification why the in-kind LCC shall not be disallowed on account of Management’s failure to obtain the consent of the DSWD on the change of the original object of the agreement that is provision of equipment and various materials and tools with a total value of ₱289,515.34;
observations.managementAction: Justification/Explanation submitted on July 31, 2024

observations.recommendation: Submit a Certification from the General Services Officer/Property Custodian that the equipment committed by the Agency were unserviceable/ non-functional during the implementation of the sub-project;

Obtain, if warranted, the approval of the DSWD on the conversion of the in-kind LCC, from provision of equipment and various materials and tools to 114.89 cubic meters excavated sand; Otherwise, the original items, as committed, shall be delivered by the Agency to avoid exposure to risks of non-compliance and breach of agreement;
Obtain proof that the 114.89 cubic meters excavated sand valued at ₱289,515.34 effectively reduced the amount due to the contractor of the project; and

Provide an explanation/ justification why the in-kind LCC, 114.89 cubic meters excavated sand with a total value of ₱289,515.34, shall not be disallowed on account of the apparent violation of the laws and regulations prohibiting quarrying in the Province of Batanes. Henceforth, ensure that environmental rules and regulations are strictly observed and implemented by the Municipality.

Provide an explanation/ justification why Management did not initiate actions against the contractor to enforce the performance of its obligation on account of the delayed completion of the Phase I of the project;

Identify the most appropriate course of action to ensure the completion of the project which includes the possible termination of the contract as justified by the circumstances. Should Management opt to terminate the same, the procedures set out in Annex I of the Updated 2016 Revised IRR. shall be strictly followed; and

Ensure that the applicable calibrated actions in response to delays in the implementation of infrastructure projects provided GPPB  Circular No. 03-2019 dated March 08, 2019 are performed specifically when the negative slippage reaches 5%, 10% or 15%;

Henceforth, require the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of the Agency’s programs, projects and activities and ensure the protection of government interest by availing of the remedies available and allowed by the Procurement Law and other regulations;
Intensify the coordination and monitoring of commitments made by  the partner agencies to facilitate the provision of the needed operational support; and

Assess the condition of the facility, the equipment and items therein and ensure the preservation of the building and all equipment and items thereon. Accordingly, repair works shall be undertaken prior to the commencement of its operations.
Continue to monitor the implementation of the PPAs to attain 100% completion;

Ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned by rigidly screening PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF especially during the preparation of budget proposal. Accordingly, require the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready;

Require the BAC and the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to properly plan all its procurement activities, particularly the procurement of infrastructure projects taking into consideration the period of implementation to avoid delays and suspensions;

Recommend to the MPDC, in collaboration with the relevant implementing offices and in coordination with the BAC, to conduct regular reviews of project implementation. This will facilitate the identification of projects that may not be feasibly implemented within the year, allowing for proactive measures and strategies to enhance fund utilization;

Direct the implementing offices to refrain from proposing projects that are fully composed of labor cost which is contrary to the intent of the Local Government Code. Accordingly, require the reviewing bodies, the Local Finance Committee and the Local Sanggunian, to rigorously evaluate the propriety, composition, accuracy and completeness of each Department’s budget proposals, to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented out of the 20% DF; and

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are already due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end

Require the implementing office, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee, to adopt a more dynamic procurement strategy aligned with weather forecasts and historical data on weather patterns in the locality to ensure the sufficiency and immediate availability of LDRRM-related items, when needed. Accordingly, for welfare food items, Management shall endeavor to enter into Framework Agreement with prospective suppliers and “Call-off” may be executed and issued to the winning supplier immediately upon the determination that an item is needed, pursuant to the guidelines under Annex 32 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act. With this, lean and just-in-time procurements are achieved and the limited storage facilities of the LGU can be maximized;

Submit explanation/ justification with verifiable basis why disbursements on procurements which were noted as non-compliant with existing procurement law, as mentioned above, should not be suspended/disallowed in post-audit;

Henceforth, require the BAC to submit the proof of validation on the technical, legal and financial capability of all the suppliers together with Mayor’s Permit and proof of PhilGEPs Registration on all purchases made through Alternative Mode of Procurement to support eligibility of suppliers;
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Certification from the BAC Secretariat as to the inclusion of the procured good and the end user's PPMP & APP was already implemented mid of 2022.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursements for the procurement of goods and services are included and updated in the end-user PPMP and Agency APP by requiring a certification to that effect from the Head of the BAC Secretariat, who shall, based on the submissions of the end-user units, validate such fact;
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Certification from the BAC Secretariat as to the inclusion of the procured good and the end user's PPMP & APP was already implemented mid of 2022.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all regulatory permits, registrations and licenses are available and up-to-date prior to engaging the services of individuals and businesses. If unavailable, encourage the prospective suppliers to secure permits and comply other registration requirements such as Mayor’s Permit, BIR and PhilGEPS registrations etc., to ensure eligibility in future procurement endeavors with the LGU;
observations.managementAction: All departments were advised to submit/attach supporting documents as to the eligibility of the non-winning bidders.

observations.recommendation: Carefully review the most appropriate mode of procurement to be undertaken to promote economy of time and resources and protect the Management from possible risks.
observations.managementAction: The BAC members were informed and will be more vigilant in  future  procurements

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to require the officials and employees of the LGU claiming traveling expenses to submit complete travel documents set forth under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and further updated by COA Circular 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 duly signed by the appropriate approving authorities, to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Updated the existing checklist of documents used in reviewing travel claims to ensure adherence to existing guidelines

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the strict implementation and enforcement of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances;

Require the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and the PPE records of the Accounting Office. Investigate and evaluate the differences, adjust the records as warranted. Henceforth, a reconciliation with the Accounting Office shall be made prior to the submission of the report to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical count of the 11 PPE Items identified to determine the condition of the property lodged in the respective accounts and submit to the Office of the Auditor for further evaluation.

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Henceforth, rigid screening of PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF shall be employed to ensure that only relevant, responsive, and feasible items are to be funded and implemented;

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the Local Finance Committee, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to determine, on a timely basis, the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated for other development projects under the 20% DF, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation;

Direct the OIC-Municipal Engineer, during the budget preparation for the ensuing year, to prepare the Program of Works (POWs) for infrastructure projects prior to inclusion of the PPAs in the Annual Investment Program (AIP);

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Accounting Office, to fast track the preparation of necessary documents for claims that are due and demandable, thereby increasing the utilization rate of the PPAs as of year-end

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the NFS the contingent liability arising from the undelivered POs at year-end to ensure the fair presentation of the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide in the NFS for the year-ended December 31,2023 the required disclosures as detailed and presented in the Revisions/Remarks column above and in conformity with the requirements of IPSAS 1 to enhance the understandability and reliability of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to require the officials and employees of the LGU claiming traveling expenses to submit complete travel documents set forth under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and further updated by COA Circular 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023 duly signed by the appropriate approving authorities, to preclude the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate journal entries in recording the net adjustment totaling ₱0.155 million, duly supported by verifiable evidence such as schedules and documents. Relative to this, all valid book reconciling items shall be recorded at year-end to enhance the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts and prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, prepare consolidation working papers and entries to eliminate in full the balances of reciprocal accounts in the Agency’s consolidated Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management: Provide an explanation/justification on the zero percent completion rate as of December 31, 2022 of the project “Rehabilitation of Water Mainline at Brgy. Savidug” funded out of CY 2021 20% DF.

Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee, to observe the timely procurement activities to ensure prompt delivery of materials and other items necessary in the implementation of PPAs, Accordingly, Management shall endeavor to conduct early procurement activities pursuant to the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act;

Provide an explanation/justification as to a.) the 95-day gap between the receipt of funds and the commencement of procurement procedures and b.) why it awarded the contract to F. Casalme Construction and Supply despite the apparent prohibition to disburse the funds beyond December 31, 2022 provided in LBC No. 131 dated January 08, 2021;
Exercise prudence in the utilization of fund transfers with due consideration on the fund validity and period of implementation of the related project from procurement up to completion;

Require the contractor to submit a Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) and direct the Agency’s Inspectorate Team to thoroughly review and validate the same. If the value of work accomplished is lesser than the amount of advance payment made, require the contractor to either refund the value of unfinished portion or continue the works to cover in full the 15% advance payment, whichever is more practicable;

Initiate the contract termination procedures pursuant to the guidelines provided in Annex I of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. A terminal report shall be submitted to the Audit Team for further review and verification.

Require the Local Disaster Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to review the existing PPAs under the LDRRMF to determine the relevance and responsiveness of the projects to the present circumstances including the reasonableness of project costing and make the necessary revisions as warranted subject to the appropriate approvals;

Instruct the LDRRM Officer to include in its LDRRMFIP  for the current year, in a separate section, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years of the Special Trust Fund;
Require the LDRRM Officer to observe the submission deadlines of the Report on the Utilization of DRRM Funds utilizing the prescribed format; and

Direct the parties involved in the preparation of the LDRRM budget to observe the documentary requirements and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for the implementation of programs, projects and activities

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to encourage the government officials and employees to settle immediately the disallowances as well as ND with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: There was no settlement made during the CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the journal entry, duly supported by documents, to reflect the adjustments on the Cash in Bank balance. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct Calendar Year (CY) 2021 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2021 comparative financial statements; and
advise the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for the accounts under the General Fund and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification.

We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO-Designate, Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the inventory count report and the accounting records. Investigate and evaluate the differences, adjust the records as appropriate and submit a revised RPCPPE based on the reconciliations made;
direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards and record the acquisition, description, custody, disposal and other information relative to the assets; and
prioritize the strict implementation and enforcement of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts and prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, prepare consolidation working papers and entries to eliminate in full the balances of reciprocal accounts in the Agency’s consolidated Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the suggested journal entry relative to the errors in asset recognition and double recording of transactions. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2021 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2021 comparative financial statements; and
advise the Municipal Accountant to review the nature of each transaction with due consideration on the asset and/or expense recognition principles set by the applicable IPSAS and COA rules and regulations and utilize the correct account titles.

We recommend that Management:
direct the Department Heads to exercise prudence in determining whether or not an activity necessitates the procurement of meals and snacks. Accordingly,  prior to the preparation of the Purchase Request,  an Activity Request duly approved by the Municipal Mayor shall be prepared showing the linkage of the activities to the specific programs/projects of the office as shown in the Annual Investment Program (AIP), expected output/s to be produced from the activities, and financial requirements and other resources and other information that may support the validity of the charging for meals and snacks;
direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and/or end-user units to properly accomplish the Request for Quotation (RFQ) and provide all important details such as specific food items/menu sought to be procured, the date of delivery/actual activity and other necessary information which may aid the bidder in accomplishing the form and the BAC in determining the most responsive bid; and
advise the Municipal Accountant to ensure that sufficient documents to establish the propriety and to evidence the actual occurrence of an activity are attached to  the Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended that Management require the identified officials and employees to file an application for bonding with the Bureau of the Treasury-Batanes Provincial Office, strictly monitor the expiration of bond coverage and apply for renewal thereof prior to its expiration as required in Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019 with due consideration on the processing time from the application to the approval of the bond.

We recommended that Management  direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to transmit to the Office of the Auditor, within five days after the execution of the contract, all documents to support the perfection thereof as  enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. A list of the documents submitted shall accompany the transmittal for ease of verification. Accordingly, a copy of the COA-received transmittal shall form part of the integral supporting documents in the processing of the related disbursement vouchers.

We recommended that Management:
direct all individual service providers under Job Order who are deriving income below P250,00.00 to execute an Income Payee’s Sworn Declaration of gross receipts/sales (Annex B2 of RR 11-2018 or Annex A1 of RMC 51-2018) and pay the Annual Registration Fee the proofs of which shall be submitted to the Municipal Human Resource Management Office;
require the authorized representative of the Agency, after submission by the service providers of the duly accomplished declaration, to execute the Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration (Annex C of RR 11-2018) and submit together with the list of payees who shall not be subjected to withholding taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
require the Municipal Human Resource Office to strictly observe the annual compliance deadlines as prescribed by RR 11-2018. Accordingly, the requirements shall be submitted by the individual service providers before January 15 or prior to initial income payment, whichever is applicable. The documentary requirements of the Agency as withholding agent shall be submitted to the BIR  office on or before the last day of January of each year or on the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month when a new income recipient submitted the payee’s sworn declaration to the lone income payor/withholding agent.

We recommended Management to strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds otherwise resort to the withholding of their salaries in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: While Management has issued demand letters, the Unliquidated Cash Advances has increased by  P44,478.50 which signifies the inability to strictly enforce collection/liquidation thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting and Treasury Offices adhere to   Section 7.2 of  COA Circular 2009-006 and certain provision of PD 1445 on the prompt submission of reports and documents.
observations.managementAction: Management submits its accounts on a timely basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned officer to insure all of the municipal properties particularly the motor vehicles with the General Insurance Fund of GSIS, otherwise provide/submit necessary documents to justify the action of not insuring some of the motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Serviceable government vehicles have been insured with the GSIS. No insurance coverage was availed for the unserviceable motor vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to adhere strictly the rules and regulations mentioned above in the initial recognition of Inventory accounts in order to reflect the correct amount of  Inventory and Expense accounts in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: While purchased supplies and materials were charged initially to Inventory accounts, these are charged immediately to expense upon release to end-users.
observations.reason: Restated in Paragraph 2, Part II-Observations and Recommendations of the CY 2021 Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management that the remaining unutilized amount on Programs/Projects/Activities which have already been completed must be returned to the source agencies as agreed upon in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and to adhere strictly to the above mentioned laws, rules and regulation.
observations.reason: Fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to analyze the occurrence of the abnormal balances of the programs, projects and activities under the account Due to NGAs and effect the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction:
Supplemental Feeding replenishment was already made. Thus, abnormal balance of P16,691.00 was eliminated.

2014 HFEP-It had been planned to request for the downloading of the remaining amount but per advice of the DOH Regional Accountant, fund was already reverted to the National Treasury.

offices concerned in the preparation of complete and updated report of government PPAs and submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor.
We recommended and management agreed that management prepare and submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Auditor for proper review and evaluation.

We recommended and management agreed that the Accountable Officer (AO), the LCE and all other officials who are responsible in the custody, possession and control of the agency’s funds must secure bond regularly in compliance to existing government rules and regulations. Management should not grant cash advances  and authorize to handle collections and deposits to AOs not bonded as disbursing and liquidating officer in order to ensure that government funds are secured and insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to stop the practice of allowing Job Order employees the grant of per diem on official travels without legal basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to ascertain the existence, actual condition and validity of the Property, Plant and Equipment account as reflected in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The RPCPPE for the year 2018 was submitted on April 24, 2019, hence delayed submission. Considering that this is an annual report, the CY 2019 RPCPPE is not yet submitted in the office of the auditor.

observations.recommendation: 1.5 We recommended Management to (a) draw the necessary adjusting entries with respect to the account Due from LGUs to ensure fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements; (b) require strict compliance in accordance with the provisions stipulated in the MOA; and (c) demand immediate submission of the liquidation reports pertaining to the funds transferred or cause the return of unused balances to the source agency and to submit official receipt to acknowledge payment thereof.

3.6 We recommended management to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to ascertain the existence, actual condition and validity of the Property, Plant and Equipment account as reflected in the financial statement.

4.3 We recommended that grant of professional fees to the Doctor to the Barrio in the succeeding months shall be withheld with the corresponding tax taking into consideration compliance with the documentary requirement in determining the applicable rate as stipulated in the provisions of Section 2 of RR No. 11-2018.
5.3 We recommended that the LCE direct all concerned department heads to submit to the Auditor their action plans and status of implementation in the 2017 AAR observations and recommendations.

6.5 We recommended management to strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds otherwise resort to the withholding of their salaries in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.

7.7 We recommended management to ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the purpose of achieving desirable socio-economic development and environmental management results and that expenditures partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures pursuant to the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

8.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, together with the members of the Municipal Waste Management Board adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9003 to ensure the protection of the public health and environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

9.5 We recommended that the Municipality fast track the implementation of all ADM/AM projects funded by the national government agencies to ensure that the desired benefits/services could be delivered by the Municipality’s constituents on time.
10.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the noted difference in the reciprocal accounts and effect necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balance as of year-end.

11.6 We recommended that the municipality disburse its LDRRM funds pursuant to NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002. The fund is intended for disaster-related activities. Likewise, adhere strictly to the procurement process of RA 9184.

We recommended management to strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds otherwise resort to the withholding of their salaries in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances were being continuously monitored for the prompt liquidation of the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to issue an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to the person having custody and control of the property/equipment to determine the accountability and facilitate the accounting thereof.
observations.managementAction: Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment to some procured equipment were issued, however there were few which were not supported with the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the purpose of achieving desirable socio-economic development and environmental management results and that expenditures partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures pursuant to the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Management has not fully utilized its 20%DF to PPAs prescribed under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to discontinue the payment of repairs on equipment at a cost exceeding 30% of its market value and to strictly adhere on the above mentioned guidelines to avoid possible issuance of disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Payment of repairs on equipment at a cost exceeding 30% of its market value were discontinued by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer or the concerned official to submit regularly Quarterly Government Projects showing the status of PPAs for implementation during the year to monitor the accomplishments made thereon to adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly report on government project were submitted, but not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to (a) follow strictly the approved Program of Work in the implementation of its projects, (b) utilized the funds intended to specific purpose/project and avoid  any disbursements not related to the project such as administrative expenses, and (c) to adhere strictly with the above cited rules and regulations to avoid possible issuance of disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Approved POW were strictly followed by management in the implementation of its projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal government submit its GPB for the ensuing year to the DILG office for review and approval pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. The GAD Focal Person is advised to monitor and evaluate the implementation of programs and activities of the approved GPB.
observations.managementAction: Management have submitted its GPB to the DILG office for review and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the acting Municipal Accountant to prepare a journal entry voucher to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in a Special Trust Fund in order to ensure and facilitate the continued use of the fund for disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP within the next five years.
observations.managementAction: Management has transferred the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in a Special Trust Fund in order to ensure and facilitate the continued use of the fund for disaster risk reduction and management activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the persons liable/responsible for the audit disallowances and suspensions to settle their accounts within the timeframe as prescribed by law and make the withholding of payment of salary and other money due to the persons liable for disallowances already served with COA Order of Execution for the settlement of their liability.
observations.managementAction: Management is continuously deducting settlements of disallowances through salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the required list of PPAs at the beginning of the year to the Office of the Auditor. Likewise, comply with Section 2.2 of the same circular by posting notification to the public.

We recommended the Municipal Agriculturist Officer to prepare and submit inventory report on livestock for monitoring purposes and an accomplishment report relative to the organic crops production, copy furnished the COA office, to evaluate the veracity of the obligations and the effectiveness of said program.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of IRR of RA 9184 on the preparation of Annual Procurement Plan as a basis in the budget preparation and COA Circular No. 97-002 in the payment of government transactions. The Local Chief Executive must require all heads of offices to submit procurement plan of their supply requirements for the ensuing fiscal year to be incorporated in the APP prepared by the GSO. Whenever practicable, consolidate purchase requests for commonly used office supplies needed by the different offices and adopt bulk purchases to enable the municipality to avail of substantial discounts.

We recommended management to strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds otherwise resort to the withholding of their salaries in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.

We recommended that management cause immediate settlement of unliquidated cash advances. The Municipal Accountant must adhere strictly with the rules and regulations as mentioned in Section 1.1.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended that management dispose all unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from the disposal.

DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

4.4 We recommended management to  discontinue  the  payment  of  repairs  on
equipment at a cost exceeding 30%  of  its  market  value  and  to  strictly
adhere on the above mentioned  guidelines  to  avoid  possible  issuance  of
disallowances.

5.7 We recommended that the Local  Chief  Executive  require  the  Municipal
Engineer or the concerned official to submit regularly Quarterly  Government
Projects showing the status of PPAs for implementation during  the  year  to
monitor  the  accomplishments  made  thereon  to  adhere  strictly  to   the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

6.5 We recommended that the LCE direct all  concerned  department  heads  to
submit to the Auditor their action plans and  status  of  implementation  in
the 2016 AAR findings and recommendations.

7.6 We recommended management to (a) follow strictly  the  approved  Program
of Work in the implementation  of  its  projects,  (b)  utilized  the  funds
intended to  specific  purpose/project  and  avoid   any  disbursements  not
related to the project such as administrative expenses, and  (c)  to  adhere
strictly with the above  cited  rules  and  regulations  to  avoid  possible
issuance of disallowance.

8.7 We recommended that the municipal government  submit  its  GPB  for  the
ensuing year to the DILG office for review and  approval  pursuant  to  PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01. The GAD Focal Person is  advised  to  monitor
and evaluate the implementation of programs and activities of  the  approved
GPB.

9.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare monthly the  Report
on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF as basis of  the  LDRRMO  to  submit
the said report to concerned offices on the required date, pursuant  to  the
above mentioned rules and regulations.

10.6 We recommended the acting Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  a  journal
entry voucher to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of  the  LDRRMF
in a Special Trust Fund in order to ensure and facilitate the continued  use
of the fund  for  disaster  risk  reduction  and  management  activities  as
provided in the LDRRMFIP within the next five years.

11.6 We recommended the  Inventory  Committee  to  conduct  annual  physical
inventory and to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property,  Plant
and Equipment within the  prescribed  period  to  ascertain  the  existence,
actual condition and validity of the Property, Plant and  Equipment  account
as reflected in the financial statement.

12.7 We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive,  together  with  the
members of the Municipal Waste  Management  Board  adhere  strictly  to  the
provisions of RA 9003 to ensure the protection  of  the  public  health  and
environment of the Municipality’s constituents.

13.10 We recommended management to require  the  persons  liable/responsible
for the audit disallowances and suspensions to settle their accounts  within
the timeframe as prescribed by law and make the withholding  of  payment  of
salary and other money due to the persons liable for  disallowances  already
served with COA Order of Execution for the settlement of their liability.
We recommended that management prepare and submit the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Auditor for proper review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted only its report as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9003. Likewise, prepare and submit detailed Work and Financial Plan of the budgeted SWMP.
observations.managementAction: Management already drafted its 10 year solid waste management plan for review by the Environment Management Bureau (EMB).

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of public funds.
observations.managementAction: Management stamped “PAID” disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-389 dated November 3, 1992.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the persons liable/responsible for the audit disallowances and suspensions to settle their accounts within the timeframe as prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction: Management is continuously deducting settlements of disallowances through salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of the MOA between the municipality and the PGB. Likewise, record the receipt of the fund transfer in the book of accounts using the correct account classification.
observations.reason: Management did not submit the required monthly

observations.managementAction: Management employed eco-aides to the different barangays to work and maintain proper garbage segregation, collection and disposal
observations.reason: No effective work and financial plan was set. No appropriat ion either for the implementa tion of RA 9003.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that management submit the required list  of  PPAs  at
the beginning of the year to the  Office  of  the  Auditor.  Likewise,
comply with Section 2.2 of the same circular by  posting  notification
to the public.

2.8 We recommended the Municipal Agriculturist  Officer  to  prepare  and
submit inventory report on livestock for monitoring  purposes  and  an
accomplishment report relative to the organic crops  production,  copy
furnished the COA office, to evaluate the veracity of the  obligations
and the effectiveness of said program.

3.3 We recommended that management prepare and submit the Report  on  the
Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Auditor for proper  review
and evaluation.

4.8 We recommended that management adhere strictly to the  provisions  of
RA 9003. Likewise, prepare and submit  detailed  Work  and  Financial
Plan of the budgeted SWMP.

5.7 We recommended that management adhere strictly to the  provisions  of
IRR of RA 9184 on the preparation of  Annual  Procurement  Plan  as  a
basis in the budget preparation and COA Circular  No.  97-002  in  the
payment of government transactions. The  Local  Chief  Executive  must
require all heads of offices  to  submit  procurement  plan  of  their
supply requirements for the ensuing fiscal year to be incorporated  in
the APP  prepared  by  the  GSO.   Whenever  practicable,  consolidate
purchase requests for commonly used  office  supplies  needed  by  the
different offices and adopt bulk purchases to enable the  municipality
to avail of substantial discounts.

6.7  We  recommended  management  to  strictly  monitor  and  cause   the
liquidation of all  outstanding  cash  advances  to  avoid  misuse  of
government funds otherwise resort to the withholding of their salaries
in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the  provisions  of  COA
Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.

7.3 We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly  comply  with  the
provisions of COA Circular 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control
in the disbursement of public funds.

8.6 We recommended that management require the persons liable/responsible
for the audit disallowances and suspensions to settle  their  accounts
within the timeframe as prescribed by law.

9.7  We  recommended  that  management  cause  immediate  settlement   of
unliquidated cash  advances.  The  Municipal  Accountant  must  adhere
strictly with the  rules  and  regulations  as  mentioned  in  Section
1.1.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

10.5 We recommended that management dispose all unserviceable  properties
to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income  from
the disposal.
